message:
chore(beads): close frankenjax-5qbt as rejected, record frankenjax-tu77 F32 AD zero/one fix

frankenjax-5qbt — Fast-path batched-operand vmap gather
- Closed as rejected after same-target Criterion A/B against
  the existing generic per-row slice/eval/stack fallback. The
  proposed flattened operand/index direct path regressed both
  shared-indices and batched-indices workloads:
  - Shared indices: ~33 ms direct vs ~6.9 ms fallback
    (~5x slower)
  - Batched indices: ~40 ms direct vs ~15.7 ms fallback
    (~2.5x slower)
- close_reason captures the regression and notes that the
  scaffolded `if false && ...` guard (landed under
  c953184/.../the-prior-scaffold-commit) keeps the implementation
  in the tree as compile-checked dead code; no behavior change.
  Removing the scaffold cleanly is its own follow-up; left as-is
  per "no destructive cleanup without explicit permission" in
  the project AGENTS.md.

frankenjax-tu77 — Preserve F32 literals in AD zero/one helpers
- New issue (priority 2, type bug, status in_progress) — fj-ad
  zeros_like/ones_like still widen scalar and tensor F32 tangents
  to F64 even though native F32 literal parity is present
  elsewhere (BatchTrace fast paths, type_promotion lattice).
- Acceptance: preserve F32Bits and F32 tensor dtype for zero/one
  tangent seeds when the helper mirrors the primal dtype, add
  focused JVP/zero-gradient tests, and validate fj-ad plus
  workspace gates (rch cargo test/check/clippy + fmt).

Beads-only commit; the implementation lands separately.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
